title: Termination law and at-will exceptions — Connecticut
target_keywords: connecticut termination law and at-will exceptions 2026

high_concern_flags.detail: Prompt asserted Conn. Gen. Stat. § 31-71c requires final pay 'by next regular payday for both voluntary and involuntary'. Verified against primary source (LawServer codified statute, CT Employment Law Blog January 2026, Pullman & Comley): § 31-71c actually requires payment by the NEXT BUSINESS DAY for involuntary discharge; voluntary quit pays on the next regular payday. Page corrected. Source: https://www.lawserver.com/law/state/connecticut/ct-laws/connecticut_statutes_31-71c

Prompt asserted CT mini-WARN at Conn. Gen. Stat. § 31-51n for 100+ employee mass layoffs/relocations. Verified against primary source (Justia codified statute, CGA Chapter 557 Part II): § 31-51n is the DEFINITIONS section; the substantive Connecticut state-law duty is at § 31-51o, which is a HEALTHCARE CONTINUATION statute (not an advance-notice statute) requiring 120 days of employer-paid group health continuation for affected employees of a 100+ employee covered establishment that closes or relocates out of state. Connecticut has NO general state mini-WARN advance-notice statute; federal WARN is the only mandatory advance-notice requirement. Page corrected throughout, including in dedicated 'What is the § 31-51o 120-day healthcare continuation obligation' section and 'mass-layoff-warn' section. Source: https://law.justia.com/codes/connecticut/2023/title-31/chapter-557/section-31-51o/

CT PFML 2026 wage base discrepancy in compliance cache ($184,500 per CTPLA / Prudential vs $176,100 per SSA October 2025 announcement). Page does not surface this specific number to readers, so no user-facing risk; flagged for cache awareness.
